title: Pistachio Price Shock Spurs South Africa Farmers to Boost Output
summary: South African farming group Karoo Pistachios is ramping up output to capitalize on surging prices and compete with the world’s biggest producers.

title: Australia Debt Office Urged to Boost Staffing, Lower Cash Buffer
summary: Australia’s sovereign debt manager should bolster staffing and consider lowering its large cash buffer to maintain best-practice as issuances grow, according to an independent Treasury-commissioned review that outlined potential risks to the nation’s funding strategy.

title: Baltic States Urge Allies Not to Go Soft on Russia
summary: The three countries have shifted economically and warn that the threat from Moscow is still very much alive.

title: Singapore Home Prices Jump Beyond Estimates on Sales Flurry
summary: Singapore private home prices rose more than initially estimated in the first quarter, as a rush for new projects continued to buoy the local market.

title: Warburg Pincus-Backed PDG Is Said to Tap Goldman for Stake Sale
summary: Asian data center operator Princeton Digital Group has tapped Goldman Sachs Group Inc. to work on a strategic review that may result in a stake sale, according to people familiar with the matter.

summary: Chinese defense contractors made a tour de force showing at an exhibition in Kuala Lumpur.

title: BYD Car Discounts Show China’s EV Price War Is Getting Worse
summary: China’s efforts to cool its automotive price war are faltering as BYD Co. and rivals expand discounts to avoid ceding ground in the world’s largest car market.

title: China Hit Brakes on Fiscal Stimulus as Economy Holds Up Amid War
summary: China pulled back on fiscal spending in March as the economy rebounded at the start of the year despite disruptions caused by the war in Iran.

title: China Allows Global Funds to Trade Government Bond Futures
summary: China will allow foreign investors to trade government bond futures from Friday, the latest in a series of steps to attract global capital to its debt market.

title: Tech Bulls Are Taking Charge of the Stock Market
summary: Nasdaq 100 is leading the rebound from the March lows.

title: Petro Speeds $7 Billion Pension Transfers, Risking Market Shock
summary: Colombia’s government is pressing ahead with a plan to transfer about $7 billion from private pension savings into the public retirement system, a move that risks destabilizing local markets as funds may be forced to rapidly liquidate assets to comply.

title: ECB to Hike in June Before Reversing in 2027, Poll Shows
summary: The European Central Bank will respond to the Iran war by raising interest rates once — in June — before reversing the move next year to protect economic growth, analysts say.

title: War Squeezes Global Mining as Diesel and Acid Supplies Tighten
summary: From the Australian outback to Ethiopia and the Democratic Republic of Congo, the global mining industry is beginning to feel the effects of disruption caused by the war in Iran.

title: Trump Suggests He’d Be Open to Alternative Probe of Fed’s Powell
summary: President Donald Trump said that he’d be open to alternative ways to investigate Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, potentially offering an off-ramp to a current legal battle that is holding up the confirmation of the president’s new pick to lead the central bank, according to news outlet Semafor.

title: Nasdaq Futures Gain on Tech Results, Oil Climbs: Markets Wrap
summary: Tech shares rallied on the back of strong results from Intel Corp. and SAP SE, with the Nasdaq 100 on track for a fourth straight weekly gain. Brent rose as the standoff between the US and Iran showed no sign of easing.

title: European Stock Returns Are Now Losing Against the US This Year
summary: European stocks have lost their outperformance edge over the US market as traders worry about the toll of persistently high oil prices on European economic growth.

title: Kazimir Says Slight ECB Rate Increase May Be Necessary on War
summary: European Central Bank Governing Council member Peter Kazimir suggested the Iran war might necessitate an eventual interest-rate hike.

title: Oil Heads for Weekly Jump as US-Iran Impasse Keeps Hormuz Halted
summary: Oil rose for a fifth day as a deadlock between the US and Iran further clouded the outlook for the Strait of Hormuz to reopen.

title: Top Indian Private Credit Borrower Delays Paying 20.75% Bond
summary: When you’re getting a 20.75% yield on a bond, you mustn’t expect all smooth sailing.

title: Repsol, BP Accused of Market Abuse by Spanish Fuel Firm Rival
summary: Repsol SA, BP Plc and slate of other oil firms have been accused of abusing market dominance and anti-competitive practices in a complaint filed by a Spanish wholesaler of refined fuels.

title: China Puts Export Curbs on First EU Firms Over Taiwan Arms Sales
summary: China added seven European entities to its export control list over their involvement in arms sales to Taiwan, marking the first time Beijing has deployed the trade blacklist against companies in Europe.

title: ECB Moves to Smooth Digital Euro Rollout With Standards Deals
summary: The European Central Bank moved to minimize adoption costs and encourage early coordination among those involved in its digital euro project, part of efforts to get the currency up and running this decade.

title: Global Bonds Set for Worst Week in a Month as US-Iran Risks Rise
summary: Global bond markets are heading for their worst week in a month as investors grow increasingly uneasy about a stalemate between the US and Iran.

title: Ifo's Fuest Says Germany Faces Risk of 'Stagnation'
summary: Ifo President Clemens Fuest says Germany could be "at the beginning of a stagflationary shock" after the institute's expectations index dropped to 83.3 in April from a revised 85.9 the previous month — the lowest level since August 2023. He speaks on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fitch Cuts Mozambique’s Rating, Citing Probable Bond Default
summary: Fitch Ratings cut its assessment of Mozambique’s foreign-currency debt by one notch to CC, saying a restructuring of the nation’s sole dollar bond had become probable.

title: German Business Outlook at Worst Since 2023 on Iran War Hit
summary: Germany’s business outlook deteriorated more than expected, adding to fears that higher energy costs stemming from the Iran conflict will derail the country’s economic revival.

title: Horizons Middle East & Africa 4/24/2026
summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Yara Boosts Profit as Iran War Sends Fertilizer Prices Soaring
summary: Fertilizer giant Yara International ASA posted higher-than-expected first quarter earnings as the Iran war effectively halted transit through the Strait of Hormuz, hurting global trade of crop nutrients and pushing up prices.

title: China’s First Special Bond Sale of the Year Draws Strong Demand
summary: China saw strong demand at its first ultra-long special bond sale of the year, with 30-year yields reaching the lowest level since a November auction.

title: One by One, Europe’s Safest Government Bonds Fall From Grace
summary: The traditional hierarchy of debt in Europe is facing its latest shake-up as worsening public finances in Belgium threaten to turn some of the region’s once-safest bonds into a risky bet.

title: NIO President on Profitability, Iran War and Price War
summary: In an exclusive interview, NIO Co-Founder and President Lihong Qin speaks with Bloomberg about the company's profitability outlook. He also discusses the ongoing EV price war, the Iran war's impact, and China's broader EV landscape. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Europe’s LNG Imports Set for First Monthly Drop in Over a Year
summary: Europe is facing its first monthly drop in seaborne supply of natural gas in over a year, with volumes dampened by terminal works and tighter global flows.

title: Schlegel Says Vigilant SNB Unrestricted on Rates, Interventions
summary: The Swiss National Bank is closely monitoring fallout from the Iran war and can steer the franc’s exchange rate without limits if necessary, according to President Martin Schlegel.
